GAZA RELIEF DISPATCH
The Need Photographs How It Helps Give Now
Gaza Strip — Dispatch, July 2026

The pots go up
before the food does.

At crowded curbs and collapsed buildings, families hold out pots and buckets for a single ladle of lentils — often the only meal of the day. The line rarely gets shorter.

Help Feed a Family Read the Dispatch
1.6M
people in Gaza facing crisis-level hunger or worse
77%
of Gaza's population affected by acute food insecurity
~800K
of them are children
Source: IPC Gaza Strip Acute Food Insecurity Snapshot, Dec 2025 – Apr 2026; World Food Programme, 2026.
01

A fragile pause, not an end

When a ceasefire took hold in October 2025, food and aid deliveries briefly increased, and the famine officially confirmed in Gaza Governorate that summer was pushed back. For a few months, bakeries reopened and markets saw more supply.

Renewed regional escalation since late February 2026 has closed most border crossings again. Four governorates remain classified in Emergency-level hunger, prices are climbing, and aid convoys are getting through in a fraction of the numbers they were.

Only about half of Gaza's hospitals and clinics are even partially functioning. Clean water is scarce enough that most households report water insecurity every week.

A queue for a hot meal forms beside a collapsed building, Gaza Strip.
02

At the kitchens

Five frames from the same story, repeated at communal kitchens across the strip: a crowd, a fence, a pot too small for the need in front of it.

FRAME 01
Pressed to the fence, pot in hand, waiting for the ladle to reach them.
FRAME 02
Two vats on an open fire, hundreds of containers waiting in line.
FRAME 03
The line begins right beside what used to be someone's home.
FRAME 04
Buckets queue along the wall before their owners even reach it.
FRAME 05
Reaching past each other for whichever bowl gets filled first.
03

How a gift helps

FOOD

Hot meals & food parcels

Community kitchens and bakeries are often the only reliable meal a displaced family gets in a day. Funding keeps the pots on the fire and the flour coming in.

WATER

Clean water & hygiene

Trucked water, repaired pipelines, and hygiene kits cut down on the disease that spreads fast through crowded shelters and tents.

HEALTH

Medical & nutrition care

Therapeutic food for malnourished children and support for the roughly half of Gaza's hospitals and clinics still standing.

04

Give through a trusted partner

This page doesn't process payments directly — your gift goes further through organizations already operating on the ground in Gaza, with the logistics and access to actually deliver it. Each link below opens the organization's own donation page.

World Food Programme
Runs community kitchens, food parcels, and bakery support reaching over a million people a month in Gaza.
Donate to WFP →
UNRWA
The UN agency for Palestine refugees — food and cash assistance, healthcare, and shelter across Gaza.
Donate to UNRWA →
Palestine Children's Relief Fund
Medical care and hospital rebuilding focused specifically on children.
Donate to PCRF →
UNICEF
Clean water, malnutrition treatment, and cash assistance through hundreds of distribution points.
Donate to UNICEF →
Gaza Relief Dispatch
Built to point people toward organizations verified to be operating in Gaza. Not itself a registered charity or payment processor.
Photographs show displacement and communal food distribution in Gaza, 2024–2025, sourced from wire and press photography. Statistics: IPC Integrated Food Security Phase Classification (Dec 2025 snapshot, projection through April 2026); World Food Programme situation updates, 2026.
